Thought 3: The narrative does not explicitly place Eddie at Letti's cabin on the night of the murder. Eddie was performing at his studio when Detective Winston visited, and he mentioned spending nights there or camping. There is no direct evidence linking Eddie to the crime scene. In contrast, Delilah was confirmed to have been with Letti at her cabin on the last night Letti was seen alive, and Delilah has a strong motive (Letti's threat to expose her illicit activities) and the means (machete skills). The murder weapon was a machete, which both suspects are skilled with, but only Delilah is directly placed at the scene with the victim on the relevant night.
